formula_or_pseudocode: Keep/generated API call candidates when inserting the API result improves LM loss over the original text by a threshold; exact reward is a filtering criterion, not an RL reward.
positive_reward: lower LM loss after API result insertion
negative_reward: discard candidate API call when likelihood gain is insufficient
source_of_signal: language-model likelihood over self-supervised API-call candidates
failure_modes: API-use decision can be myopic because each call is filtered locally.; No multi-turn credit assignment or semantic task-level reward is modeled.; Bad or irrelevant API calls are removed by likelihood heuristics rather than learned from environment feedback.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: successful task completion
negative_reward: failed trajectory or wrong answer
source_of_signal: environment observations and benchmark evaluator
failure_modes: Wrong observations or early bad actions can propagate through later reasoning.; Reasoning text can be plausible even when the action path is wrong.; Prompted action format can break in harder environments.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: successful episode or useful evaluator feedback
negative_reward: failed episode converted to corrective reflection
source_of_signal: scalar or verbal task feedback
failure_modes: Reflection memory may preserve misleading explanations if feedback is weak.; Self-reflection can mask semantic errors when the evaluator is shallow.; Repeated attempts still depend on the same task-level feedback bottleneck.

formula_or_pseudocode: ToolEval pass rate = proportion of instructions completed within budget; win rate = pairwise evaluator preference over solution paths.
positive_reward: instruction completed within budget; preferred solution path
negative_reward: unsolved instruction or worse path
source_of_signal: ChatGPT-backed ToolEval plus API responses
failure_modes: LLM evaluator can disagree with humans or miss subtle API misuse.; Temporal API variability complicates reproducible execution evaluation.; Pass rate hides where in a trajectory the tool-use failure occurred.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified in current extraction; benchmark scores API call correctness and task completion.
positive_reward: correct API call and completed task
negative_reward: wrong API, wrong arguments, or task failure
source_of_signal: benchmark ground truth / API evaluator
failure_modes: Benchmark metrics can conflate syntactic API correctness with semantic task success.; Coverage of realistic long-horizon user interaction is limited compared with later benchmarks.; APIBench/API-Bank naming ambiguity can pollute taxonomy if not kept separate.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified as a unified reward; each environment supplies its own success metric.
positive_reward: environment task success
negative_reward: failed episode
source_of_signal: environment-specific evaluator
failure_modes: Aggregated benchmark scores can hide environment-specific failure modes.; Long-horizon failures may stem from early bad actions and state drift.; Evaluation-only source does not tell how to train reward-seeking policies.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified as a generic formula; tasks are judged by functional success criteria.
positive_reward: web task completed according to evaluator
negative_reward: wrong final website state or failed task
source_of_signal: WebArena task evaluator / environment state
failure_modes: Surface navigation may look plausible while failing the user goal.; Long web trajectories are vulnerable to early wrong clicks and state drift.; Functional success is sparse and gives little credit assignment.

formula_or_pseudocode: Reward/evaluation analogue: pass if generated patch resolves the issue under tests; fail otherwise.
positive_reward: all required tests pass / issue resolved
negative_reward: test failure, patch failure, or unresolved issue
source_of_signal: repository test execution
failure_modes: Tests can be incomplete, so passing tests may not guarantee semantic correctness.; Long code-edit workflows have sparse terminal feedback.; Agents can overfit to visible tests or exploit benchmark artifacts.

formula_or_pseudocode: r = r_action * r_output in {0,1}; pass^k estimates probability that all k independent trials succeed.
positive_reward: correct database outcome and necessary user-facing information
negative_reward: wrong action, missing output, policy violation, or failed task
source_of_signal: domain policy checks, database state, simulated user episode
failure_modes: Wrong information to the user and wrong domain-policy decisions are observed failure classes.; Pass^1 can overstate reliability because pass^k drops when behavior is inconsistent.; Binary terminal reward is sparse for multi-turn API transactions.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: correct final math answer
negative_reward: wrong final answer or invalid tool execution
source_of_signal: math benchmark answer checker and tool execution
failure_modes: Correct-looking tool traces can still yield wrong final answers.; Execution helps arithmetic but does not solve planning/semantic mistakes.; SFT on trajectories may not teach recovery from novel tool failures.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: higher agent task score
negative_reward: lower task score
source_of_signal: AgentInstruct / agent benchmark evaluation
failure_modes: Instruction tuning can improve average behavior without explicit credit assignment.; Benchmark aggregation can hide which agent skills fail.; No reward design for online correction is provided.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: correct QA answer and useful trajectory
negative_reward: wrong answer or poor trajectory
source_of_signal: QA benchmark metrics
failure_modes: Fine-tuning may imitate trajectories without learning when to recover.; Search observations can be misread or over-trusted.; QA correctness remains sparse at the final answer level.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: correct function/tool call under benchmark evaluator
negative_reward: wrong function, argument, or invalid call
source_of_signal: function-calling benchmarks
failure_modes: Function-call accuracy can miss long-horizon task success.; Format correctness and semantic correctness may be conflated.; SFT action models may not adapt to unfamiliar tools.

formula_or_pseudocode: not specified
positive_reward: accurate function call and higher benchmark score
negative_reward: incorrect generated call or lower benchmark score
source_of_signal: tool-learning data pipeline and function-calling evaluator
failure_modes: Generated data quality can overfit to benchmark formats.; Function-call syntax can improve without robust semantic tool use.; No online reward signal for recovery from tool errors is reported.

formula_or_pseudocode: Accept generated sample iff format_check && execution_check && semantic_check.
positive_reward: passes all verification stages
negative_reward: fail format, fail execution, or fail semantic check
source_of_signal: JSON parser, real function execution, LLM semantic checker
failure_modes: Format and execution checks filter many errors but semantic checker quality still matters.; Execution success alone may not imply the user intent was satisfied.; Generated data may inherit API/library coverage bias.

formula_or_pseudocode: Pipeline accepts blueprints/trajectories after format checks, execution checks, policy unit tests, and LLM review; exact scalar reward not specified.
positive_reward: valid executable blueprint and high-quality simulated trajectory
negative_reward: format, execution, policy-test, or review failure
source_of_signal: environment execution, unit tests, LLM reviewers, simulated human
failure_modes: Simulated human can drift over turns.; Generated trajectories depend on verifier and reviewer reliability.; Blueprint correctness may not cover all realistic conversational variants.

formula_or_pseudocode: r = outcome_correctness(final answer) during multi-turn code-execution rollout; exact scalar scale not specified in note extraction.
positive_reward: correct final answer after tool-integrated reasoning
negative_reward: wrong final answer
source_of_signal: AIME/math answer checker with code execution observations
failure_modes: Outcome-only reward is sparse for long tool-use trajectories.; Bad code execution can compound unless the model learns self-correction.; Models can misuse tools or fall back to brittle heuristics without RL exploration.

formula_or_pseudocode: r = outcome_success(task) for trajectories with interleaved reasoning, tool queries, and tool outputs; detailed formula not specified in extracted text.
positive_reward: successful final answer/task outcome
negative_reward: failed solution/task
source_of_signal: task/benchmark outcome
failure_modes: Outcome-only RL may leave tool-use credit assignment unresolved.; Tool failures or hallucinated reasoning can be hidden until final evaluation.; Function-calling benchmarks may reward surface call correctness more than robust recovery.

formula_or_pseudocode: StarPO views rollouts as state-thinking-actions-reward trajectories and optimizes trajectory-level rewards; exact implementation formula not summarized here.
positive_reward: higher environment trajectory reward
negative_reward: failed trajectory / unstable learning
source_of_signal: environment reward across multi-turn rollouts
failure_modes: Echo Trap: reward variability cliffs and gradient spikes.; Without fine-grained reasoning-aware rewards, agents may learn shallow strategies or hallucinated thoughts.; Rollout diversity and interaction granularity affect stability.

formula_or_pseudocode: Outcome reward assesses correctness of final response; exact-match is used in experiments unless otherwise noted.
positive_reward: correct final answer after search interactions
negative_reward: incorrect final answer
source_of_signal: rule-based answer evaluator / exact match
failure_modes: GRPO may converge faster but can become unstable or collapse after many steps.; Outcome-only rewards give little signal about query quality.; Retrieved irrelevant information can mislead reasoning.

formula_or_pseudocode: R_final = R_format + R_correct; R_format in {0,1}; R_correct = 6*(R_max/S_max)-3 over name, parameter-name, and parameter-value matching.
positive_reward: valid format and matched tool names/parameters/values
negative_reward: invalid format or mismatched tool call components
source_of_signal: rule-based matching against ground-truth tool calls
failure_modes: Coarse correctness rewards are sparse for multi-component tool calls.; Overweighting format can lead to format learning without task correctness.; SFT initialization can yield high training rewards but weaker generalization.

formula_or_pseudocode: AST/subtree matching is used as scalable proxy for function execution; exact category formulas vary by BFCL section.
positive_reward: correct function invocation under BFCL evaluator
negative_reward: wrong function, wrong arguments, irrelevant call, or failed agentic task
source_of_signal: BFCL AST/executable/relevance evaluators
failure_modes: AST/function-call correctness may miss deeper task semantics.; Benchmark versions vary over time and must be cited carefully.; Single-turn strength does not imply memory, dynamic decision-making, or long-horizon competence.

formula_or_pseudocode: Q^pi(s_t,a_t)=E[sum_{k=t}^T gamma^{k-t} r(s_k,a_k)|s_t,a_t]; train PRM on Monte Carlo Q targets; update policy to maximize Q_phi(s,a)-beta KL(pi||pi_prev).
positive_reward: high PRM Q estimate and successful rollout return
negative_reward: low Q estimate or failed rollout
source_of_signal: Monte Carlo rollouts, outcome rewards, expert demonstrations for InversePRM
failure_modes: Reward hacking: PRM score can increase while outcome success drops.; PRM accuracy degrades under distribution shift if policy moves too far.; Sparse outcomes make exploration and sample efficiency hard.

formula_or_pseudocode: DMPO loss increases likelihood of preferred trajectory tau_w and decreases likelihood of dispreferred tau_l with discounted/length-normalized trajectory log-ratio terms.
positive_reward: preferred/winning trajectory with higher final reward
negative_reward: dispreferred/losing trajectory
source_of_signal: trajectory pairs from final rewards in agent environments
failure_modes: Behavior cloning suffers compounding errors in dynamic environments.; Noisy preference data and trajectory length disparity affect optimization.; Trajectory-level preferences still provide limited local credit assignment.

formula_or_pseudocode: R_rho(F,T,P) scores alignment of proposed output P with feedback F and task T; outputs are ranked to create chosen/rejected preference pairs for EPO.
positive_reward: output ranked high by environment-feedback reward model
negative_reward: incorrect output ranked low
source_of_signal: visual/interaction feedback and learned reward model
failure_modes: Reward model quality depends on grounding environment feedback.; Adjacent embodied setting may not transfer directly to API/tool-use agents.; Long-horizon subgoal errors can propagate to low-level actions.

formula_or_pseudocode: LLM feedback score S(t,o) is used to train a reward model; RL fine-tunes the lightweight model against that reward model. Exact scalar formula is partly extracted but not fully normalized here.
positive_reward: higher AI feedback score and executable/correct API code
negative_reward: low feedback score or non-executable/wrong code
source_of_signal: larger LLM feedback plus code metrics
failure_modes: Narrow code-generation setting may not represent broad tool-agent RLAIF.; AI feedback quality depends on the larger LLM prompt.; Executability can improve while semantic API appropriateness remains incomplete.

formula_or_pseudocode: Checklist item reward fires when an item switches from unsatisfied to satisfied with dependencies met; trajectory reward aggregates weighted checklist rewards across turns/steps/items into R in [0,1].
positive_reward: binary checklist item satisfied with evidence grounding
negative_reward: unsatisfied checklist item or failed criterion
source_of_signal: GPT-5 checklist annotation and LLM-as-judge over rollout prefixes
failure_modes: Dense step assignment can amplify judge noise.; Checklist generation and judging may introduce stochasticity.; Wrong tool information is an explicit failure example in checklist prompts.

formula_or_pseudocode: r = 1 iff the agent successfully fulfills the task in accordance with the system prompt; r = 0 otherwise.
positive_reward: task successfully fulfilled
negative_reward: task not fulfilled
source_of_signal: task outcome in multi-turn user/tool environment
failure_modes: Binary reward is sparse but avoids direct exploitation of syntax rewards.; Dynamic users make rollouts stochastic and harder to optimize.; Dialogue requirements can impede learning correct tool invocation patterns.

formula_or_pseudocode: Example outcome reward: 1 if exact match and format correct, 0.2 if answer wrong but format correct, -1 if format incorrect. Intermediate reward = retrieval reward + format reward + search-count penalty.
positive_reward: correct retrieval/tool execution, correct format, correct final answer
negative_reward: bad format, excessive search, wrong final answer
source_of_signal: exact match, format verifier, retrieval verifier, LLM judge
failure_modes: Trajectory-level reward aggregation causes poor credit assignment.; GRPO with turn-level advantages can require exponential rollout samples in general K-turn settings.; Verifiable rewards are rigid; LLM-as-judge is more flexible but may be noisy.

formula_or_pseudocode: Single-turn reward r_piGT(s,a) in {0,1} indicates whether action matches expert trajectory action for state; GRPO improves this single-turn reward and lower-bounds multi-turn success.
positive_reward: action matches expert trajectory / successful planning step
negative_reward: non-expert or wrong action
source_of_signal: expert trajectories and environment success
failure_modes: Depends on expert trajectories and unique optimal action assumptions.; Sparse episode rewards and long-horizon credit assignment motivate the decomposition.; Noisy non-expert trajectories can degrade performance, though GRPO shows robustness.

formula_or_pseudocode: Binary reward/accuracy: 1 if PRM selects the correct action over the plausible incorrect alternative, else 0.
positive_reward: correct action ranked above plausible incorrect action
negative_reward: incorrect action ranked above correct action
source_of_signal: offline/online sampled tool trajectories and multi-LLM verification
failure_modes: Early mistakes propagate through long tool-use sequences.; Weak PRMs can amplify incorrect trajectories during reward-guided search.; Label noise requires multi-LLM verification and filtering.

formula_or_pseudocode: Stage-1 final reward = retrieval reward + 0.5 format reward. Stage-2 reward = F1 answer reward + format reward, where bad format receives -2.
positive_reward: valid retrieval invocation, correct format, high F1 answer match
negative_reward: bad format, no useful retrieval, low answer F1
source_of_signal: retrieval count/format verifier and answer F1
failure_modes: Format reward had to be refined to avoid reward hacking and abnormal outputs.; Retrieval reward alone can teach search invocation without answer correctness.; EM reward can shorten responses and underperform F1 reward.

formula_or_pseudocode: J(theta)=E_{s in T, a~pi_theta(s)}[R(a|s)], where R(a|s) comes from a generative reward model over each step context.
positive_reward: high generative reward for context-aware next action
negative_reward: low reward for poor action/sub-trajectory
source_of_signal: Gemini 1.5 Pro generative reward model over synthetic trajectories
failure_modes: Generative reward model can be overly permissive or harsh.; Incorrect intermediate steps compound across multi-step tasks.; Offline synthetic data quality and filtering strongly affect performance.

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; UProp estimates inherited/extrinsic uncertainty from preceding decisions using trajectory-dependent decision processes.
positive_reward: lower propagated uncertainty can support safer continuation or human review routing
negative_reward: higher propagated uncertainty flags risky continuation or possible inherited error
source_of_signal: information-theoretic uncertainty estimator over multi-step agent decisions
failure_modes: Uncertainty estimates are diagnostic rather than directly corrective.; Mapping uncertainty to reward penalties requires calibration.; Not API/function-calling specific.

formula_or_pseudocode: Hierarchical advantage estimation assigns credit at planner/subgoal level and executor/action level by aggregating returns over subgoal execution.
positive_reward: subgoals and actions that improve long-horizon return
negative_reward: subgoals/actions with low return or failed execution
source_of_signal: environment returns decomposed through hierarchical policy structure
failure_modes: Requires meaningful subgoal decomposition.; Not specific to API transaction semantics.; Hierarchical reward can hide low-level semantic errors if subgoals are wrong.

formula_or_pseudocode: Find first irrecoverable step with binary-search rollout trees; apply hierarchical advantage attribution and stronger corrective updates to the critical step and suffix.
positive_reward: localized correction of first irrecoverable step and successful recovery trajectory
negative_reward: early critical mistake and suffix actions depending on it
source_of_signal: rollout-tree localization plus tool execution outcomes
failure_modes: Localization quality depends on rollout tree reliability.; Not all API errors are irrecoverable or detectable by suffix failure.; Tool setting is mainly TIR/code/math/science.

formula_or_pseudocode: LLM post-hoc critic refines step-level Q-values through hindsight reasoning; multi-scale advantage supplements intermediate baselines.
positive_reward: steps judged useful under hindsight and successful long-horizon outcomes
negative_reward: steps with poor hindsight Q estimates or failed outcomes
source_of_signal: post-hoc LLM critic plus environment outcomes
failure_modes: Post-hoc critic can introduce model bias.; Hindsight can rationalize spurious steps.; Requires enough rollout evidence for delayed reward attribution.

formula_or_pseudocode: Macro advantage from complete trajectory groups plus micro advantage from actions grouped by repeated anchor states across trajectories.
positive_reward: actions that outperform alternatives from comparable anchor states
negative_reward: actions that underperform comparable state-group alternatives
source_of_signal: group-based relative advantage over trajectories and anchor-state groups
applies_to: multi-turn LLM agent RL and search QA
failure_modes: Requires identifying comparable states across rollouts.; Sparse environments may have few repeated anchors.; Micro advantages can be noisy when state matching is weak.

formula_or_pseudocode: Belief-consistency supervision converts discrepancies between predicted beliefs and observed feedback into dense signals; belief-aware grouping estimates advantages under similar belief states.
positive_reward: beliefs consistent with later observations and successful actions under partial observability
negative_reward: belief drift or belief-observation mismatch
source_of_signal: dense self-supervised belief consistency plus delayed task reward
failure_modes: Very recent source; belief-state extraction may itself drift.; Belief consistency does not guarantee correct external action.; API-specific irreversible writes remain under-covered.

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; benchmark labels hallucination presence, responsible step, and causal explanation for multi-step agent trajectories.
positive_reward: correctly identifies hallucinated trajectory and responsible step
negative_reward: misses or misattributes the silent/hallucinated step
source_of_signal: human-curated trajectory annotations
failure_modes: Attribution remains difficult and tool-use hallucinations are especially challenging.; Labels can support verifier research but are not direct reward optimization.

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; tool call is allowed only if precondition holds and state is committed only if postcondition verifies the result.
positive_reward: verified invocation and verified state transition
negative_reward: blocked precondition or failed postcondition
source_of_signal: Hoare-style tool contracts and runtime verification
failure_modes: Contract coverage determines what can be caught.; Open-ended semantic success still needs domain or business oracles.

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; lightweight classifiers over internal representations detect tool-calling hallucinations during generation.
positive_reward: early detection of incorrect tool selection or parameter-level hallucination
negative_reward: undetected wrong tool
source_of_signal: malformed parameter
applies_to: model internal representations and labeled hallucination cases
failure_modes: generation / call
column11: Requires access to internal model states.; Detector confidence must be calibrated before being used as a reward penalty.

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; failed parameter filling is categorized across tool-agent invocation chains and can be converted into verifier tests.
positive_reward: parameter consistency and correct argument filling
negative_reward: parameter name hallucination
source_of_signal: wrong source mapping
applies_to: input perturbations and tool-agent failure taxonomy
failure_modes: parameter / call
column11: Taxonomy is diagnostic.; Reward use requires building strict parsers

formula_or_pseudocode: not a training reward; SimpleToolHalluBench measures hallucinated tool calls when no tool or only distractor tools are available.
positive_reward: refuses unavailable tools and selects only grounded available tools
negative_reward: hallucinates unavailable or distractor tools
source_of_signal: diagnostic benchmark over no-tool and distractor-tool settings
failure_modes: Reasoning-enhancement risk evidence
confidence: not a mitigation.; Reliability-capability trade-off requires new objectives beyond format reward.
